messages.content:
Describe a mitigation strategy for this type of vulnerability.

A firmware update vulnerability exists in the fw_check.sh functionality of Wavlink AC3000 M33A8.V5030.210505. A specially crafted HTTP request can lead to arbitrary firmware update. An attacker can perform a man-in-the-middle attack to trigger this vulnerability.

Mitigation should include implementing secure firmware update mechanisms, such as cryptographic signatures to verify firmware integrity (CWE-307). Additionally, using HTTPS with certificate validation can prevent MitM attacks. Regularly updating firmware and employing network traffic monitoring can also help.
